CS200: The ANSI 107 Class 2 Mesh Tee solves the vest-over-shirt problem — the certified visibility is the shirt. Nearly 900 printed here across 34 orders; road, utility, and site crews wear it all day in summer because mesh breathes where a vest layer bakes. Logos print without touching the certification.
CSV105: The Class 2 Surveyor Vest is the pocketed professional's vest — zippered front and a full workstation of pockets for tools, phones, and paper. Five hundred-plus decorated; survey crews, inspectors, and supers who live in their vest pick this one over the economy models every time.
CS413: The Women's Select Snag-Proof Polo posted 118 separate orders — the most reorder-dense women's garment in our uniform program. Same industrial-grade snag-proof knit as the men's CS412, tailored properly. Medical, security, and service teams keep it in constant rotation.
CSF300: The Class 3 Heavy-Duty Fleece Full-Zip is certified visibility you can actually stay warm in — a real fleece jacket carrying full ANSI Class 3 coverage. Three hundred-plus decorated for winter road and utility crews; the logo goes on without touching a stripe.
CSJ24: The Class 3 Waterproof Parka is the serious end of hi-vis — 65 separate orders for the coat that keeps certified visibility through a full Northwest winter storm. Waterproof, insulated, taped for Class 3. When the crew works regardless, this is the coat.
CSJ501: The Class 3 Waterproof Insulated Jacket covers the worst shifts of the year — certified visibility, real insulation, waterproof shell. Thirty-eight separate orders, almost all winter road and utility crews. Decoration is placed around the taping so the rating stays intact.
CSJ500: The Class 3 Economy Waterproof Insulated Jacket makes certified winter hi-vis affordable at crew scale — 65 separate orders prove the price point works. Waterproof, insulated, fully taped; the budget answer that still passes every site check.
CS430: The CornerStone Workwear Pocket Tee is the heavy-duty pocket tee for crews that destroy standard shirts — 69 separate orders. It's cut for work, built for industrial laundry, and the pocket carries what pockets carry. Prints and embroidery both hold up.
CSJ25: The Class 3 Safety Windbreaker is the light shell of the certified lineup — full ANSI coverage in a wind-shedding layer for spring and fall shifts. It packs small, wears easy, and takes a printed logo cleanly on the solid panels.
CSV102: The Class 2 Mesh Zippered Vest upgrades the classic site vest with a real zipper — no hook-and-loop wearing out, no flapping front. Three hundred moved here fast; it's the everyday certified vest for crews that want one step nicer than economy.
CS409: The Class 3 Long Sleeve Snag-Resistant Tee combines full-coverage certified visibility with a knit that survives job-site abuse — 300-plus printed, mostly in fleet batches. It's the shirt-as-vest answer for crews that need Class 3 without layers.
CS201: The Class 2 Mesh Long Sleeve Tee extends the breathable certified tee into sleeve coverage — sun protection and visibility in one shirt. Two hundred fifty-plus printed; summer road crews order it alongside the short sleeve.
CSW175: The Short Sleeve Select Ripstop Shirt is the modern work shirt — ripstop toughness in a uniform cut, ready for shop or field. It's the step-up option in our workwear shirt programs; embroidery over the chest pocket reads classic.
CS418: The Select Lightweight Snag-Proof Polo is the summer version of our most durable uniform polo — same snag-proof integrity, lighter hand. Nearly 300 embroidered; teams already in CS410s and CS412s add it for the warm half of the year.
CS208: The Class 3 Long Sleeve Tee with segmented tape trades the stiff stripe for a segmented reflective pattern that flexes with the shirt — certified visibility that moves. Crews that complain about crackly tape pick this one.
CS202: The Class 3 Mesh Tee is full-coverage certified visibility in a breathable knit — 348 printed across 32 orders. The step up from Class 2 for higher-speed corridors, in a shirt crews will actually keep on in July.
CSF301: The Class 3 Heavy-Duty Fleece Pullover is the hooded counterpart to the certified fleece jacket — pullover warmth with full ANSI coverage. Winter site crews order it where a zip front just means cold in.
CSJ503: The Class 3 Soft Shell brings the modern soft-shell feel to certified hi-vis — wind-blocking stretch with full taping. It's the jacket for crews that find parkas too much and windbreakers too little.
CSV101: The Class 2 Economy Mesh Zippered Vest is the fleet-scale answer — certified visibility, zip front, by-the-box pricing at a 71% margin. Nearly 300 decorated in short order; visitors, subs, and new hires get vested without a budget meeting.
CSJ41: The Washed Duck Cloth Insulated Hooded Work Jacket is CornerStone's take on the classic duck work coat — broken-in feel, real insulation, hood included, at a sharper price than the big-name equivalent. Thirty-two separate orders; embroidery on duck reads timeless.
CSJ502: The Class 3 Waterproof Ripstop 3-in-1 is the do-everything certified system — waterproof shell, removable liner, three jackets in one garment. Twenty-one separate orders for crews that face every season on the same corridor.
CS203: The Class 3 Mesh Long Sleeve Tee is the full-coverage certified summer shirt — mesh breathability, long-sleeve sun protection, ANSI Class 3 rating. Utility and highway crews order it for the exposed months.
CSV104: The Class 2 Mesh Six-Pocket Zippered Vest is the working supervisor's vest — certified visibility plus six pockets of real capacity. It carries phones, pens, tape, and paper without a tool belt; decoration rides the solid trim.
CSB815: The CornerStone Tactical Duffel is built like the workwear it travels with — rugged construction, MOLLE-style webbing, gear-scale capacity. It took a single 110-unit order here; crews and agencies gift it logoed.
SY60: The Red Kap Short Sleeve Solid Ripstop Shirt is fleet-mechanic standard issue — ripstop durability in the classic industrial uniform cut. Two hundred seventy-plus embroidered with company marks and name patches; it's shop-floor uniform done right.
PC78: The Core Fleece Crewneck posted 130 separate orders — the most-reordered sweatshirt in our records. It's the plain, perfect crew: soft fleece, clean chest, full-roster price. Big printed graphics and left-chest embroidery both live happily on it.
PC78Q: The Core Fleece 1/4-Zip Pullover brings the quarter-zip silhouette to sweatshirt fleece — 750-plus decorated. It's the warm version of the office quarter-zip: collar-up polish, hoodie comfort, budget-friendly at team scale.
PC90ZH: The Essential Fleece Full-Zip Hooded Sweatshirt is the zip cut of the budget hoodie standard — 65 separate orders. Crews that live in the PC90H pullover order this for easy on-off over layers; same fleece, same decoration, zipper convenience.
PC55P: The Core Blend Pocket Tee adds the trades' favorite feature to our most-printed blend tee — nearly 900 moved here. Cotton-poly durability, real chest pocket, and a print-friendly face; we decorate over the pocket and it reads uniform instantly.
PC90: The Essential Fleece Crewneck is the heavier-duty crew sweatshirt — the same dense fleece as the PC90H hoodie without the hood. Five hundred-plus decorated across 37 orders; shops and warehouse crews that layer under jackets pick the crew.
PC61P: The Essential Pocket Tee is the pocket version of our heavyweight cotton workhorse — 63 separate orders. Dense cotton that survives industrial laundering, plus the chest pocket crews ask for. Print or embroider beside it and it's a uniform.
PC61T: The Tall Essential Tee posted 82 separate orders — proof that tall sizing isn't a niche, it's a necessity. Full length in body and sleeve so the heavyweight cotton classic fits crews over 6'2". Ordered alongside regular PC61s, printed identically.
PC147: The Tie-Dye Tee moves in event volume — 500-plus printed across 42 orders. Real spiral tie-dye under your printed design reads festival, fundraiser, and summer camp. Dark solid prints sit best over the swirl.
PC61LSP: The Long Sleeve Essential Pocket Tee stacks all three requests — heavyweight cotton, long sleeves, chest pocket — into one shirt. Sixty-one separate orders, nearly all trades. It's the fall-through-spring uniform tee for working crews.
PC450Y: The Youth Fan Favorite Tee brings the soft, retail-feel tee to kid sizes — 60 separate orders, a school and league staple. It prints beautifully for spirit wear and matches the adult PC450 for family and team sets.
BP78: The Core Fleece Sweatshirt Blanket is literally made of sweatshirt — the same soft fleece as the region's favorite crews and hoodies, in stadium-blanket form. An embroidered corner logo makes it one of the most-kept gifts we produce.
PC145: The Crystal Tie-Dye Tee is the subtler wash — a crystal-dye texture that reads boutique rather than summer camp. Three hundred-plus printed; brands wanting the dyed look without the full spiral land here.
PC79H: The Port & Company Fleece Pullover Hooded Sweatshirt is the new heavyweight in the family — a denser, warmer hoodie a step up from the everyday PC78H. Its first big order here moved 220 units at once; expect it in winter crew programs.
LPC55: The Women's Core Blend Tee is the tailored cut of the most-printed shirt in our shop — same easy-printing blend, real women's fit, at an 81% margin. Ordering PC55s? Split the run with LPC55s and the whole crew actually fits.
PC54TT: The Core Cotton Tank Top is the summer cut of the catalog's classic tee — same cotton, same colors, no sleeves. Nearly 300 printed for gyms, crews, and summer events; it takes bold prints exactly like its parent PC54.
